title: ervilia cassubica
intro: Ervilia cassubica, also known as the Polish milkwort or cassubian ervilia, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ervilia cassubica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate sinus pressure, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. Ervilia cassubica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used topically to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
